skill.name: delon-acl
skill.description: Guidance for implementing @delon/acl role and permission checks in Angular applications, including UI visibility, route guards, and server-side enforcement reminders.
skill.summary: Implement @delon/acl role-based access control in Angular apps.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged dynamic imports, shell-style backticks, weak cryptography keywords, and system reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown documentation examples and substring matches, with no executable scripts, network calls, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Angular Lazy-Loading Imports
low_findings.description: The dynamic import findings are Angular route lazy-loading examples inside a TypeScript documentation block. They do not execute during skill installation or skill activation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports appear only in a route configuration example within SKILL.md. No runnable package file, script entry point, or generated execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Backticks
low_findings.description: The shell backtick findings correspond to Markdown code fences and inline documentation examples, not Ruby or shell command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited location is part of Markdown formatting around TypeScript, template, or rules examples. There is no Ruby file, shell script, or command execution construct.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings match descriptive text about access control and the @delon/acl package. No cryptographic algorithm is used or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe role-based access control and package purpose. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings occur in Angular component examples and placeholder method bodies. They do not call operating system discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are ngOnInit and empty UI handler examples. I found no evidence of whoami, uname, id, host enumeration, or similar reconnaissance commands.

content.value_statement: Angular teams need consistent role and permission checks across routes, components, and data access. This skill explains how to use @delon/acl with guards, UI visibility, and server-side rule reminders.
actual_capabilities: Explains @delon/acl roles, abilities, and ACLService usage.
Shows how to hide Angular UI elements with ACL directives and permission checks.
Provides route guard patterns for role-based and ability-based access.
Maps blueprint membership roles to application permissions.
Reminds teams to pair client checks with Firestore Security Rules.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install @delon/acl.
Examples are tailored to Angular, ng-alain, and @delon/acl version 20.1.0.
Client-side ACL examples must not replace backend authorization.
Project-specific services and repositories must be adapted by the user.

use_cases.title: Add role checks to Angular screens
use_cases.description: Use ACLService and ACL directives to show actions only for users with matching roles or abilities.

use_cases.title: Protect admin and edit routes
use_cases.description: Create route guards that redirect unauthorized users to an access denied page.

use_cases.title: Align UI permissions with data rules
use_cases.description: Connect client permission checks with Firestore Security Rules for stronger enforcement.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic ACL check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a component permission check.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delon-acl skill to add a simple @delon/acl role check to my Angular component. Explain the role and ability names I should define.

prompt_templates.title: Hide actions by permission
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delon-acl skill to update this Angular task detail screen so edit and delete actions only appear for allowed users.

prompt_templates.title: Design route guards
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced route protection for protected pages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delon-acl skill to design Angular route guards for admin, task creation, and blueprint editing routes.

prompt_templates.title: Review permission architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a multi-tenant permission model.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the delon-acl skill to review my role, ability, route guard, and Firestore rule design for a blueprint-based Angular app.

output_examples.input: I need admin-only settings and task edit buttons in an Angular dashboard.
output: Define admin as a role and task:edit as an ability.
Use ACLService for component checks and ACLIfDirective for template visibility.
Add route guards for pages that must never load for unauthorized users.
Mirror destructive actions in server or Firestore authorization rules.

output_examples.input: Review whether my client ACL is enough for deleting tasks.
output: Client ACL improves usability but does not enforce security by itself.
Keep the delete button hidden for unauthorized users.
Require the same permission in backend logic or Firestore Security Rules.
Test users with owner, admin, member, and viewer roles.

best_practices: Initialize ACL after authentication and before protected routes render.
Use clear role and ability names such as admin and task:delete.
Enforce sensitive actions on the server or in Firestore Security Rules.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on hidden buttons for authorization.
Do not hardcode role checks across many components.
Do not skip tests for viewer, member, admin, and owner edge cases.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps implement role and permission checks with @delon/acl in Angular applications.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready authorization by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance, and users must adapt examples to their project.

faq.question: Can client-side ACL protect sensitive data?
faq.answer: No. Client-side ACL controls visibility, but sensitive data needs server-side enforcement.

faq.question: Which Angular pattern does it support?
faq.answer: It covers ACLService, ACLIfDirective, route guards, Angular signals, and Angular control flow examples.

faq.question: Is it specific to one application?
faq.answer: Some examples reference a blueprint-based app, but the ACL patterns can be adapted.

faq.question: Why did the audit mark static findings as false positives?
faq.answer: The flagged patterns appear in Markdown documentation examples, not executable skill code.
